title: passerina salsolifolia
intro: Passerina salsolifolia, also known as Desert Mallow, is a plant species native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It is a perennial shrub with showy pink to purple flowers and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, providing various health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and protecting against oxidative stress. Desert Mallow can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to soothe wounds, calm digestive issues, and support immune function.
